vignettes.title: Disjunctive Forest Fire
vignettes.description: A match lit or a lightning strike causes a forest fire.

M.notes: 1 if the match is lit, 0 if not

FF.notes: 1 if forest fire occurs, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: The forest fire occurs if either the match is lit or a lightning strike happens.

vignettes.title: Conjunctive Forest Fire
vignettes.description: A forest fire occurs only if both a match is lit and a lightning strike occur.

M.notes: 1 if the match is lit, 0 if not

FF.notes: 1 if forest fire occurs, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: The forest fire occurs only if both a match is lit and a lightning strike happen together.

vignettes.title: Bottle Shattering Scenario
vignettes.description: Suzy and Billy throw rocks at an intact bottle. The bottle shatters if either Suzy's rock or Billy's rock hits it.

ST.notes: 1 if Suzy throws a rock, 0 if not

BT.notes: 1 if Billy throws a rock, 0 if not

SH.description: Suzy's rock hits the intact bottle
SH.notes: 1 if Suzy's rock hits the intact bottle, 0 if not

BH.description: Billy's rock hits the intact bottle
BH.notes: 1 if Billy's rock hits the intact bottle, 0 if not

BS.notes: 1 if the bottle shatters, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: Suzy's rock always hits the bottle if she throws it. Billy's rock only hits the bottle if he throws it and Suzy's rock does not hit it. The bottle shatters if either Suzy's rock or Billy's rock hits it.

vignettes.title: Loader Example
vignettes.description: A prisoner dies if either A loads B’s gun and B shoots, or C loads and shoots his own gun.

A.notes: 1 if A loads B's gun, 0 if not

B.notes: 1 if B shoots his gun, 0 if not

C.description: C loads and shoots his gun
C.notes: 1 if C loads and shoots his gun, 0 if not

D.notes: 1 if the prisoner dies, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: The prisoner dies if either A loads B's gun and B shoots, or if C loads and shoots his own gun.

vignettes.title: Overdetermination in Voting
vignettes.description: Five agents vote, and a majority of 3 is required for the proposal to pass. A, B, and C vote yes, while D and E vote no.

A.notes: 1 if A votes yes, 0 if not

B.notes: 1 if B votes yes, 0 if not

C.notes: 1 if C votes yes, 0 if not

D.notes: 1 if D votes yes, 0 if not

E.notes: 1 if E votes yes, 0 if not

P.notes: 1 if the proposal passes, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: The proposal passes if at least 3 agents vote yes. A, B, and C voted yes, so the proposal passes. This creates an overdetermination scenario where no single vote is strictly necessary.

vignettes.title: Switch Scenario
vignettes.description: Flipper flips a switch to change the train's trajectory to the right track. If Flipper had not flipped the switch, the train would have taken the left track. The train's arrival depends on the track it takes.

F.notes: 1 if Flipper flips the switch, 0 if not

RT.notes: 1 if the train takes the right track, 0 if not

LT.notes: 1 if the train takes the left track, 0 if not

A.notes: 1 if the train arrives, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: The train arrives at its destination regardless of which track it takes, but the track is determined by whether Flipper flips the switch.

vignettes.title: Short Circuit
vignettes.description: The dislodgement of a boulder threatens to hit a hiker. However, the threat also provokes a reaction from the hiker – ducking – which prevents the threat from materializing.

BD.notes: 1 if the boulder dislodges, 0 if not

B.notes: 1 if the boulder rolls towards the hiker, 0 if not

D.notes: 1 if the hiker ducks, 0 if not

E.notes: 1 if the hiker gets hit by the boulder, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: The dislodgement of the boulder directly causes it to roll toward the hiker and also provokes the hiker to duck. The hiker’s ducking prevents the boulder from hitting them.
